relevant_files: data/wins.json WIN-067 (current) — no monitor.py snapshot present in raw-text/
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Dome ties aetheric pressure to disc geometry via H(r) = 8,537×exp(-r/8,619) km (05-model.txt L16). Domain 4 (live-power L67-71) publishes g(r) = 9.7803×(1+0.005307×exp(-r/λg)) with λg defined separately at L22 as 8,619 km. Domain 19 (L127-128) publishes Mascon 'Anomaly <20 mGal' one-sided threshold. L152 publishes 'Mascon Gravity ↔ SAA Boundary: r=0.92 (Sub-terrestrial structure)' correlation without mechanism equations.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Domain 4 formula coefficients (9.7803, 0.005307) and decay constant (λg = 8,619 km) are WGS84-derived — the formula evaluates at r=0 to the polar value 9.8322 m/s² by tautology. Domain 19 is not a formula but a correlation statement + one-sided magnitude threshold. Review correctly identifies this as pass-by-threshold not pass-by-prediction.
dome_code_analysis.data_sources_used: Raw-text snapshots only — we don't have a monitor.py copy to confirm whether Domain 4 / Domain 19 live monitoring is hardcoded or live-fetched. Review text asserts 'monitor code evaluates at r=0 only' — defensible inference from the formula structure (decay factor collapses), but not directly validated against source. monitoring tag currently 'none' in wins.json; if monitor.py actually contains the Domain 4 formula with a hardcoded WGS84 comparator, tag should be 'hardcoded'. Flagged as H5 (c11) below for verification when monitor.py snapshot is next refreshed.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Domain 19's one-sided threshold ('Anomaly <20 mGal') cannot be falsified if the anomaly is ≥20 mGal (the real East Antarctic Bouguer anomaly is -40 to -60 mGal). Review calls this out in detail_extra (iii). Domain 4's falsification criterion ('Model error >0.1% vs WGS84 standard') is used affirmatively against the dome in detail_evidence Patch A — at r=20015 km the 0.476% error trips the dome's own threshold by 4.76× ≈ 4.8×.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: One minor line-number imprecision flagged as H4 (c11) below: 'raw-text/08-live-power.txt line 70' is cited as the source for 'λ_g = 8,619 km', but line 70 only contains the formula (which references λg symbolically). The value λg=8,619 km is defined on L22 of the same file.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Retained from c10. No monitor.py snapshot in raw-text/ to validate; detail_evidence asserts 'monitor code evaluates at r=0 only' but no cited file path anchors this claim. Claim is inferable (the Domain 4 exponential collapses at r=0) but not directly verified. If monitor.py does carry the Domain 4 formula and compares to a hardcoded WGS84 polar value, tag should be upgraded to 'hardcoded'. Holding at 'none' pending monitor.py refresh — see H5 (c11).
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Domain 4 is the WGS84 Somigliana-style polar-gravity formula in dome notation (coefficients match WGS84 to 0.09%). Domain 19's 'Sub-terrestrial structure' label is the dome's name for the Mascon-gravity feature already attributed by standard geophysics to East-Antarctic mantle structure (Pappa 2019, Ferraccioli 2011) plus post-glacial isostatic adjustment (Shepherd 2012). The dome renames the cause; it does not produce a different predicted value.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: GRACE/GRACE-FO public since 2002 (JPL Level-2 archive); Ferraccioli et al. 2011 (Nature) and Pappa et al. 2019 (JGR) both pre-date any V5x dome 'prediction' of the Antarctic gravity low. detail_verdict_text explicitly documents the post-hoc finding and calls out the timestamp-of-prediction gap under the dome author's own definition.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: The working Domain 4 formula's coefficients are WGS84 values expressed in dome notation (tautology at r=0); the 0.5% variation is produced by the WGS84 gravity flattening coefficient k=0.005307, not a dome-geometry derivation. The H(r)-coupled prediction (the one that IS derived from dome geometry) fails by 170–1,430×. No equation linking disc radius / firmament height / aetheric parameters to the observed 9.832/9.780 gradient exists in V51.2 live-power.

holes_found.description: c10 H1 still present. Phrase 'through the associated lithospheric/mantle structure, contribute to the geometry of the SAA field minimum' overcites Pappa et al. 2019 (Antarctic crustal gravity) into the SAA field-minimum domain, which is attributed by mainstream geomagnetism to core-dynamo reverse-flux patches (Finlay 2020 IGRF; Tarduno et al.). EXP-245 queued for analyst 2026-04-24T17:29:40Z but not yet delivered. Not a new hole — re-flagged here so the c11 review record shows the defender-surface remains live until EXP-245 integrates.
holes_found.recommendation: Await EXP-245 analyst delivery. Preferred fix per c10: split citation into (a) Pappa et al. 2019 for the gravity anomaly, (b) Finlay et al. 2020 IGRF / reverse-flux-patch literature for the SAA field minimum. Alternative: soften to 'plausible co-localization through deep crustal/lithospheric heterogeneity' without claiming Pappa explains the SAA. Defer c12 spot-check of this fix until EXP-245 integrates.

holes_found.description: c10 H4 still present. The orphan close 'The refraction cascade (Section 1.8) adds a further constraint: at the Antarctic rim (r ≈ 15,500–20,015 km), the firmament ceiling H(r) ≈ 1,400–840 km.' announces a 'further constraint' and then states a height range without follow-through, leaving the reader at 'so what?'. EXP-246 queued for analyst 2026-04-24T17:29:40Z but not yet delivered. Re-flagged for record-keeping.
holes_found.recommendation: Await EXP-246 analyst delivery. c10 offered two shapes: (a) complete the thought — 'adds a further constraint: at the Antarctic rim, H(r) ≈ 1,400–840 km, so the linear-coupling prediction collapses gravity to a fraction inconsistent with WGS84 at any latitude in this range — closing the last loophole that variable H could hide the discrepancy'; (b) drop the orphan and let the prior paragraph close. Current state remains the worst of both.

holes_found.location: detail_evidence Patch A; detail_extra section (iii) closing sentence
holes_found.description: The 4.8× Domain-4-violation figure is computed only against the WGS84 polar anchor (9.8322 m/s²) and stated without a range. Using the three Antarctic-latitude anchors the review itself uses elsewhere: polar/90°S (9.8322) → 4.76×; 80°S (9.8306) → (9.8306−9.7854)/9.8306 = 0.460%/0.1% = 4.60×; 70°S (9.826) → (9.826−9.7854)/9.826 = 0.413%/0.1% = 4.13×. The polar anchor is deliberately the most damaging to the dome (highest ratio) — that's the right choice — but the single-point presentation is stylistically inconsistent with the explicit 170–1,430× range elsewhere. A defender's reasonable objection is 'why polar and not coastal?' — preemption costs ~40 chars.
holes_found.recommendation: Minor edit, not EXP-worthy. Replace the bare '4.8×' with a range-parenthetical, e.g. 'by 4.8× (4.1–4.8× across 70–90°S anchor choices; polar anchor shown here)' — one phrase, matches the review's stated preference for range presentation. Defer to next decider self-apply cycle — not urgent, not blocking.

holes_found.location: detail_evidence, second long paragraph: 'length scale λ_g = 8,619 km to be identical to the H(r) decay constant 8,619 km (raw-text/08-live-power.txt line 70 vs. raw-text/05-model.txt line 16)'
holes_found.description: Line 70 of raw-text/08-live-power.txt is the formula line 'Formula: g(r) = 9.7803×(1+0.005307×exp(-r/λg))' — it references λg symbolically. The numeric value λg = 8,619 km is actually defined at L22: 'λg = 8,619 km (Aetheric scale length)'. A motivated reader checking the citation would hit L70 and find no '8,619' value, then have to search upward. Minor, easy to fix.
holes_found.recommendation: Change '(raw-text/08-live-power.txt line 70 vs. raw-text/05-model.txt line 16)' to '(raw-text/08-live-power.txt lines 22, 70 vs. raw-text/05-model.txt line 16)' or '(λg value at L22, formula at L70 in 08-live-power.txt; H(r) at L16 in 05-model.txt)'. Minor edit; pairs well with the H3 (c11) batch.

holes_found.category: new — tag validation blocked by missing artifact
holes_found.description: The 'monitoring: none' tag is retained from c10, but detail_evidence itself asserts 'The gravity formula used in monitor.py (Domain 4 gravity-anomaly entry) is g(r) = 9.7803×(1+0.005307×exp(-r/8619))' and 'The monitor code evaluates at r = 0 only'. If that assertion is correct, the Domain 4 gravity check IS in monitor.py and the tag should be 'hardcoded', not 'none' (static formula + static WGS84 comparator, no live fetch). Curmudgeon cannot currently validate: raw-text/ has no monitor.py snapshot — only the 08 text pages. Three cases: (a) detail_evidence's monitor.py claim is correct → tag should be 'hardcoded'; (b) detail_evidence is over-reading the live-power.txt formula as a monitor.py assertion → the detail_evidence claim should be softened and tag stays 'none'; (c) monitor.py carries live-fetch against GRACE or similar → tag should be 'live_fetch'. Can't distinguish without the artifact.
holes_found.recommendation: Two-step. (1) Next poller refresh that pulls monitor.py into raw-text/ should trigger a tag-validation pass. (2) If monitor.py snapshot unavailable, soften the detail_evidence claim from 'monitor.py' to 'the dome repository's gravity pipeline' or 'Domain 4's published formula' — the tautology argument doesn't need monitor.py source access to land, only the live-power.txt formula. Minor; not blocking; record-keeping priority.

holes_found.location: detail_evidence paragraph 1 ('no such mechanism appears in V51.0') vs paragraph 2 ('as of V51.2, publish a sentence') vs detail_extra ('V51.2 live-power page', 'V51.2 live-power numbering')
holes_found.description: Review mixes 'V51.0' (ECM model-header version, as printed in 08-live-power.txt L11 'ECM V51.0 20-Domain Convergence Dashboard') with 'V51.2' (audit-statistics version, L29 'AUDIT STATISTICS (V51.2)'). Internally consistent to someone who has read the live-power page. To a reader, the text reads as if the author lost track of which version they're citing. Defensible, but a mild clarity issue.
holes_found.recommendation: Harmonize to 'V51.2' where the current live-power page is the referent, and add a single footnote-style clarification at first V51.0 mention, e.g., 'V51.0 (ECM model header; live-power page audit statistics current V51.2)'. Pair with H3/H4 (c11) as a text-only micro-patch.

advocate_mode.strongest_defender_line: You are leveraging Domain 4's '0.1% vs WGS84 standard' falsification criterion as if the dome intends that criterion to apply at every r from 0 to 20,015 km. Domain 4 in live-power.txt is titled 'Equatorial Gravity' — the falsification criterion might be scoped to a specific measurement locale (the equator), not the whole disc. Your argument that evaluating at r=20,015 km violates a 0.1% bound presupposes a global scope the dome may not have intended.
advocate_mode.our_current_rebuttal: Survives. The dome's own formula is g(r) = 9.7803×(1+0.005307×exp(-r/λg)) — a function of r, not a single-point value. If the dome intended only 'r=0 equatorial gravity', the formula would be a scalar, not a function; the inclusion of the exp(-r/λg) term only makes sense if the formula is expected to hold across a range of r. The dome's own live-power page publishes this formula as its sole quantitative gravity check — there is no separate Antarctic gravity formula. So: either Domain 4 applies at r=20,015 km (and fails by 4.8×), or the dome has no quantitative gravity prediction for Antarctica at all (which forces the critique into the Domain 19 / Sub-terrestrial-structure branch, where there is no equation to evaluate — also a loss for the defender). The conditional in detail_evidence already traps both branches.
advocate_mode.notes: Defender could also attack the 4.76× figure's polar-anchor choice (see H3 c11). Preemption cost is trivial (single phrase) and the conclusion ('fails by 4.1×–4.8× depending on anchor') is still a clear violation of the 0.1% threshold. Recommend rolling into the H3 (c11) micro-patch.

cross_win_consistency.check: WIN-067 labels itself 'the second self-contradiction documented after the H(r) chain' — referring to WIN-012/WIN-013/WIN-014 (κ denominator vanishing). Does the label hold?
cross_win_consistency.finding: Consistent. WIN-012/WIN-013/WIN-014 indeed form the first H(r)-chain self-contradiction (verified via wins.json; κ denominator collapse documented there). WIN-067 is the second because it documents a self-contradiction INTERNAL to the dome's own two gravity stories: H(r)-coupling (fails 170–1,430×) vs Domain 4 formula (WGS84 clone, matches reality). The 'second' label is accurate.
cross_win_consistency.finding_2: WIN-067's Domain 4 / Domain 19 analysis overlaps topically with WIN-025 (magnetic variations) and WIN-068 (Sq/ionosphere) — both cluster WINs with active citation audits (EXP-244 pending, EXP-247 pending). No direct citation collision with WIN-067's gravity/Pappa/Ferraccioli/Shepherd citations; the cluster audit is magnetic/ionospheric. Safe to review WIN-067 independently.
cross_win_consistency.finding_3: Section 1.8 cross-reference (refraction cascade) in the hanging sentence — the refraction cascade is the Section 1.8 derivation of H(r) ≈ 1,400–840 km at the Antarctic rim. That's internally consistent: WIN-067 uses the 837 km figure from H(20015)=8537×exp(-20015/8619)=837 km, and 1,400–840 km is the range from r≈15,500 to r≈20,015 km. So the H-range quoted in the hanging sentence matches the H(r) formula. No arithmetic inconsistency there — just prose incompleteness per H2 (c11).

Only non-issue flagged is the single-anchor presentation of 4.8× (H3 c11) — the value is correct; the opportunity is to show a 4.1–4.8× range for stylistic consistency.

kernel_of_truth.description: The r=0.92 Mascon↔SAA correlation is a real geophysical observation, and the spatial co-location of the Antarctic gravity low and the SAA field minimum is not a random accident. The dome is correctly pointing at a non-trivial regularity between two anomaly fields.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Spatial correlation does not require a dome-specific mechanism. Standard geophysics explains the co-location via shared deep-Earth heterogeneity (East Antarctic mantle plume + LGM ice-loading + core-mantle-boundary reverse-flux patches). The dome relabels the shared cause 'Sub-terrestrial structure' without supplying any equation linking dome geometry to either field. A rebranded observation is not a prediction — r=0.92 is an empirical regularity in data the dome author could have known before writing monitor.py.

review_lens_notes.tldr_review: tldr_evidence and tldr_verdict were both updated in the c10 patches. Arithmetic cross-checked this cycle: all six headline numbers (170×, 590×, 1,430×, 5,544×, 38%, 90%) verify. No TLDR factual errors. 'Conservative dimensionless lower bound' wording on 170× carries sufficient caveat for the TLDR layer even without the 'different baselines' expansion present in detail_verdict_text. No change recommended.
review_lens_notes.self_contradicted_verdict: Self-Contradicted stands. The review documents two mutually incompatible dome gravity stories — H(r)-coupled (fails 170–1,430×) vs Domain 4 WGS84-clone formula (matches reality but is globe-in-dome-notation). Either way, the dome loses. Patch A's explicit use of Domain 4's own falsification quote against it strengthens the verdict materially vs. the c8/c9 state.

summary_for_decider: WIN-067 is in good shape after the 43fa5da self-apply. c10 findings: 3 of 5 fully fixed (H2/H3/H5), 2 still open (H1 SAA overcite, H4 refraction orphan) pending analyst EXP-245 and EXP-246 delivery. Self-Contradicted verdict holds; if anything the explicit 4.76×≈4.8× Domain-4 falsification-by-own-criterion derivation strengthens it. Four new findings this cycle, all MINOR and all text-only: (H3) single-polar-anchor on 4.8× inconsistent with range style elsewhere — could say 4.1–4.8×; (H4) λg line-number imprecision (L70 → L22, 70); (H5) monitoring-tag validation blocked by missing monitor.py snapshot — either upgrade tag to 'hardcoded' if detail_evidence's monitor.py claim is correct or soften the claim; (H6) version label mix (V51.0 vs V51.2) wants a one-phrase clarification. H3/H4/H6 pair well as a single text-only micro-patch; H5 can wait for poller to refresh monitor.py. Recommend no new EXPs — all c11 findings are decider-self-apply-scale. Next c12 should spot-check EXP-245/246 integrations when they land.
